title: Feds propose ‘student loan safety net’ alongside forgiveness
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House is moving forward with a proposal that would lower student debt payments for millions of Americans now and in the future, offering a new route to repay federal loans under far more generous terms.

title: Biden, López Obrador, Trudeau meet in Mexico City for summit
shortDescription: MEXICO CITY (AP) — President Joe Biden, Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau are meeting for a series of talks on migration, trade and climate change on Tuesday as the three leaders try to mend tensions that have divided the continent.

title: DOJ reviewing potentially classified docs at Biden center
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is reviewing a batch of potentially classified documents found in the Washington office space of President Joe Biden's former institute, the White House said Monday.

title: Having elected House speaker, Republicans try governing
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Electing the House speaker may have been the easy part.

title: How Republicans are transforming the House in the majority
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lawmakers no longer have to walk through metal detectors before gaining access to the House floor. And any time they do vote, they will have to do so in person — no more voting by proxy from home.

title: House GOP kicks off majority with vote to slash IRS funding
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Republicans began their tenure in the majority Monday by passing a bill that would rescind nearly $71 billion that Congress had provided the IRS, fulfilling a campaign promise even though the legislation is unlikely to advance further.

title: Trump’s longtime CFO faces sentencing for tax fraud scheme
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Allen Weisselberg, a longtime executive for Donald Trump 's real estate empire whose testimony helped convict the former president’s company of tax fraud, is set to be sentenced Tuesday for dodging taxes on $1.7 million in job perks.

title: Sarah Huckabee Sanders to take oath as Arkansas governor
shortDescription: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Former White House press secretary Sarah Huckabee Sanders will be sworn in as Arkansas' 47th governor on Tuesday, assuming a seat her father once held and becoming the first woman to hold the post.

title: Birth control ruling to see fresh scrutiny at Texas Capitol
shortDescription: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Samantha Sorsby-Jones watched friends at her Texas high school go to great lengths to get birth control: Secretly arranging rides to clinics that didn’t require parental consent and hiding phones in bushes in case parents were tracking them.

title: Sweden sees bigger Russian threat to telecoms, power network
shortDescription: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Sweden’s SAPO domestic security agency warned Tuesday that it expects Russia to increase activities threatening Swedish security in fields including telecoms and the power network.

title: Nepal’s new PM secures vote of confidence in parliament
shortDescription: KATHMADU, Nepal (AP) — Nepal’s newly appointed prime minister secured a decisive vote of confidence in parliament on Tuesday with support from both his seven-party coalition and the opposition.Prime Minister Pushpa Kamal Dahal received the support of 268 of the 270 members who were present in the House of Representatives, the lower house of parliament, in the voting Tuesday.

title: NATO chief confident Nordic pair will join despite holdups
shortDescription: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Jens Stoltenberg expressed confidence Tuesday that Finland and Sweden will join the military alliance, just days after the government in Stockholm said it had done all it could to satisfy Turkey’s reservations about its membership.

title: ‘No amnesty!’: Brazilian protests demand jail for rioters
shortDescription: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — “No amnesty! No amnesty! No amnesty!”The chant reverberated off the walls of the jam-packed hall at the University of Sao Paulo’s law college on Monday afternoon. Hours later, it was the rallying cry for thousands of Brazilians who streamed into the streets of Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paulo, penned on protest posters and banners.

title: UN extends critical aid from Turkey to Syria’s rebel north
shortDescription: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council voted unanimously Monday to keep a key border crossing from Turkey to Syria’s rebel-held northwest open for critical aid deliveries for another six months.

title: Brazil and Jan. 6 in US: Parallel attacks, but not identical
shortDescription: Enraged protesters broke into government buildings that are the very symbol of their country’s democracy. Driven by conspiracy theories about their candidate's loss in the last election, they smashed windows, sifted through the desks of lawmakers and trashed the highest offices in the land in a rampage that lasted hours before order could be restored.

title: EXPLAINER: Roots of the Brazilian capital’s chaotic uprising
shortDescription: SALVADOR, Brazil (AP) — Thousands of Brazilians who support former president Jair Bolsonaro invaded the Supreme Court, presidential palace and Congress on Jan. 8 in an episode that closely resembled the U.S.

title: Biden, López Obrador open Mexico meetings with brusque talk
shortDescription: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Andres Manuel López Obrador challenged U.S. President Joe Biden to end an attitude of “abandonment” and “disdain” for Latin America and the Caribbean as the two leaders met on Monday, making for a brusque opening to a summit of North American leaders.

title: Ill. Senate OKs gun ban, House Democrats agree with changes
shortDescription: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Illinois Senate approved a ban on semiautomatic weapons Monday, hours after Gov. J.B. Pritzker was sworn in to a second term and expressed disgust over shootings so frequent each "needs a title so you know which one we’re referring to.”

title: New Pa. speaker wants ‘work group’ after slow session start
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A week after he was a surprise choice to become speaker of the Pennsylvania House of Representatives, Democratic state Rep. Mark Rozzi on Monday canceled sessions for the rest of the week after failing to reach a deal on his primary legislative priority.

title: Republicans challenge New Mexico redistricting after loss
shortDescription: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Attorneys for Democratic lawmakers in New Mexico urged the state Supreme Court on Monday to dismiss a Republican challenge to a congressional map that divvies up a politically conservative region of the state.

title: Washington lawmakers convene for 105-day session
shortDescription: OLYMPIA, Wash. (AP) — Lawmakers in Washington state returned to Olympia on Monday to begin a 105-day legislative session, the first one fully in person since the COVID-19 pandemic forced lawmakers and the public to often meet and vote virtually.

title: Georgia grand jury ends probe of Trump, 2020 election
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — The special grand jury in Atlanta that has been investigating whether then-President Donald Trump and his allies committed any crimes while trying to overturn his 2020 election loss in Georgia has finished its work, bringing the case closer to possible criminal charges against Trump and others.

title: 13 killed in Peru clashes amid new anti-government protests
shortDescription: LIMA, Peru (AP) — At least 13 people died Monday in southeast Peru as protests seeking immediate elections resumed in neglected rural areas of the country still loyal to ousted President Pedro Castillo.

title: Louisiana state treasurer says he will run for governor
shortDescription: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana State Treasurer John Schroder announced Monday that he is running for governor. Louisiana’s current Gov. John Bel Edwards, a Democrat, is unable to run this year due to term limits, creating a huge opportunity for the GOP to capture the state’s top government post.

title: Hobbs focuses on border, schools in 1st speech to lawmakers
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs on Monday praised President Joe Biden's weekend trip to the U.S.-Mexico border and his stepped-up efforts to combat illegal immigration.A week after the newly elected Democrat took office, Hobbs looked for balance between get-tough border hawks and immigration advocates focused on a humanitarian approach in her first state of the state address to Arizona's Republican-controlled Legislature.

title: Idaho governor touts funding for education, public safety
shortDescription: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho Gov. Brad Little announced proposals Monday for property tax relief, education funding and efforts to fight the influx of illegal fentanyl during his annual State of the State address.

title: GOP’s White files to seek reelection as Mississippi auditor
shortDescription: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — Republican Shad White has filed to seek another term as state auditor in Mississippi.White was first appointed to the job by then-Gov. Phil Bryant in 2018 after Stacey Pickering stepped down.

title: DOJ reviewing classified documents at Biden facility from time as VP
shortDescription: Richard Sauber, a special counsel to the president, said the documents were immediately turned over the National Archives.

title: How the GOP got here: Rise of hardliners from Goldwater to MAGA
shortDescription: Goldwater, Reagan, the Tea Party all made their marks but ultra-conservatives last week might have made the biggest gains in the House speaker fight.

title: Biden to discuss Ukraine-Russia war and clean energy with Canada
shortDescription: President Joe Biden and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to discuss clean energy and Ukraine. A trilateral meeting with Mexico expected Tuesday.

title: Georgia grand jury completes Trump probe
shortDescription: Fulton County Superior Court Judge Robert McBurney set a Jan. 24 hearing to determine whether the report would be made public.

title: Biden seeks Mexico's AMLO help in stopping deadly fentanyl
shortDescription: Joe Biden kicks off the North American Leaders' Summit asking Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador for help tackling the fentanyl crisis.

title: U.S. House rules approved for 118th Congress
shortDescription: U.S. House rules approved for the 118th Congress with concessions that range from reducing government spending to freedom caucus members getting more seats.

title: Six Albuquerque Democrats' homes, offices targeted by shootings: police
shortDescription: A suspect is in custody in connection with an apparent string of shootings targeting Democratic lawmakers homes and offices in Albuquerque, N.M.

title: All eyes on Georgia after grand jury wraps work on Trump investigation
shortDescription: In today's OnPolitics: Georgia grand jury completes Trump probe work, President Joe Biden visits the border and rules vote brings more Congress drama.

title: McCarthy's new GOP ditches House metal detectors in one of first acts
shortDescription: Republicans removed metal detectors outside the House floor that were erected in the wake of Jan. 6 in their first act of power last week.

title: Gaetz, Rogers make peace after House floor confrontation
shortDescription: Republican Mike Rogers created one of the viral moments of the historic speaker election last week when he charged at fellow GOP Rep. Matt Gaetz.

title: Charging decisions on Trump loom large in Atlanta. What we know.
shortDescription: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis' team of prosecutors and investigators has guided the grand jury's work since it was empaneled in May.

title: 70,000 US deaths: Can Biden, Mexico agree on fighting fentanyl?
shortDescription: President Joe Biden heads to Mexico to discuss the lethal fentanyl threat with Mexico's president. Analysts say the U.S. needs much more cooperation.

title: Despite new policies, Biden immigration plans may be tied up in court
shortDescription: As Biden heads into a summit with his counterparts from Mexico and Canada, his immigration policies are increasingly being tied up  in federal courts.

title: Biden faces pleas for help on migrant crisis as he visits El Paso border
shortDescription: Joe Biden's trip to El Paso marks his first visit to the U.S.-Mexico border since he became president two years ago.

title: From immigration to fentanyl, Biden to highlight US issues in Mexico
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is meeting with Mexico and Canada as the U.S. faces record levels of migrants and rising fentanyl overdoses.

title: How Trump could shape the race to replace Stabenow
shortDescription: Republicans say they will compete for Debbie Stabenow's open Senate seat in 2024, but will Donald Trump help or hurt that effort.

title: Biden to seek mayors' help to implement 'new opportunities'
shortDescription: President Joe Biden invites U.S. mayors to the White House on Jan. 20, his second anniversary in office, to discuss bipartisan work on key priorities.

title: In echo of Jan. 6, Brazil protesters storm their Congress, high court
shortDescription: The uprising comes after supporters of far-right President Jair Bolsonaro wouldn't accept his defeat by President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva.

title: US appeals court blocks ban on rapid-fire ‘bump stocks’
shortDescription: A Trump administration ban on bump stocks was blocked by an appeals court. The device was banned after used by the gunman in the 2017 Las Vegas attack

title: Homeless crisis, a city overwhelmed: What Biden may see at El Paso border
shortDescription: If given the chance, the president will see the migrant crisis among people sleeping under Red Cross blankets outside Sacred Heart Catholic Church.

title: McCarthy's next battle: Rallying GOP behind deal he made with hard-liners
shortDescription: GOP Rep. Nancy Mace, a staunch Kevin McCarthy supporter, said she was "on the fence" about the rules package the speaker negotiated with hard-liners.

title: How a new Congress will change the direction of the country: What we know
shortDescription: Speaker Kevin McCarthy and the GOP have control of the House of Representatives. Meanwhile, Democrats hold the edge in the Senate.

title: Slain Jan. 6 rioter's mother arrested on Capitol attack anniversary
shortDescription: Video footage of Witthoeft's arrest shows Capitol Police officers telling her and other protestors to move to the "sidewalk now, or you're under arrest."

title: Norman Pattis, Alex Jones' lawyer, loses law license for 6 months
shortDescription: Norman Pattis, Alex Jones' attorney, is also representing Proud Boy Joseph Biggs in an ongoing seditious conspiracy trial in D.C.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The Elections That Will Matter in 2023
shortDescription: Among the races to watch are governors’ contests in Kentucky, Louisiana and Mississippi and mayoral elections in Chicago and Philadelphia.By Neil Vigdor

title: House Republicans Vote to Approve Rules Package
shortDescription: Republicans united to push through the new rules, days after a protracted battle to elect Kevin McCarthy as House speaker. The package passed in a mostly party-line vote, with just one Republican voting no.By The Associated Press

title: New House Rules Make It Easier to Dump Speaker, and Harder to Spend or Raise Taxes
shortDescription: The rules package Republicans pushed through on Monday allows a single lawmaker to force a vote on “vacating” the speaker’s chair.By Carl Hulse and Luke Broadwater

title: Final Vote Count: How Each Member Voted on the House Rules Package
shortDescription: See how the House voted on the rules that govern how it operates, which include a series of concessions to a group of ultraconservative Republicans.By Allison McCartney and Emily Cochrane

title: Election Deniers Are Also Economy Deniers
shortDescription: There’s no recession yet, and inflation is falling.By Paul Krugman

title: House Republicans Vote to Rescind I.R.S. Funding
shortDescription: The measure, one of the new majority’s first legislative moves, would cut billions in funding that Democrats passed to help crack down on tax cheats. It does not have enough votes to pass the Senate.By Emily Cochrane and Alan Rappeport

title: Cherokee tribes turn to K Street to fulfill a 187-year-old promise for a seat in Congress
shortDescription: But they aren’t in agreement over who should get the largely ceremonial post.
